name: PELVIC FLOOR DYSFUNCTION
definition: heterogeneous group of disorders presenting with pelvic pain, pressure, dyspareunia, shortest distance between the most caudal aspect of an organ and H line during 0-3.5 cm; elevation during squeeze of 0-4.5 cm; elevation during weakening of pelvic floor support structures consisting of muscles and connective tissue (= abnormal protrusion of a pelvic organ(s) through its respective hiatus prolapse / herniation of bladder through its respective hiatus on anterior vaginal wall UTERINE AND VAGINAL VAULT PROLAPSE uterus prolapse completely outside of hiatus CUL-DE-SAC HERNIA herniation of peritoneal membrane protruding between uterosacral ligaments at apex of MESENTEROCELE bulging / outpouching of anterior rectal wall anterior to transverse perineus muscle into descent / infolding of mucosa or entire thickness of rectal wall through anal verge descent of the entire (full wall) thickness of the rectal wall into anal canal; starting 6-

demographics.Prevalence: 10-50% of middle-aged / older women increasing with age; 10% requiring

distribution: downward + clockwise bladder rotation ← posterior bladder wall descends
